title: Docs assistant · how a question gets answered
subtitle: What each piece does, where its data comes from, and what travels between steps: from raw docs to a cited answer in the chat.

nodes.label: Product docs
nodes.role: The knowledge base: guides and reference pages, exported as markdown.
nodes.why: This is what users ask about; everything downstream serves it.

nodes.label: Resolved tickets
nodes.role: Real questions with confirmed answers, pulled from the support queue.
nodes.why: Covers the gap between what the docs say and what users ask.

nodes.label: Curation pass
nodes.role: A human removes stale pages and marks canonical answers before anything gets indexed.
nodes.why: Garbage in the index becomes confident wrong answers.

nodes.label: Chunker + embedder
nodes.role: Splits documents into semantic chunks and turns each one into a vector.
nodes.why: The model cannot read the whole corpus per question; retrieval needs pieces it can rank.

nodes.label: Eval set
nodes.role: Golden questions with expected answers; every pipeline change runs against it.

nodes.label: Vector store
nodes.role: Holds every chunk with its vector and metadata (source page, date).
nodes.why: One queryable place for similarity search; the metadata keeps citations honest.

nodes.label: Retriever
nodes.role: Takes the user question, finds the most relevant chunks, reranks them.

nodes.label: LLM
nodes.role: Writes the answer from the retrieved context and cites the source pages.
nodes.why: Grounded generation: the model answers from the context pack, and says so when it cannot.

nodes.label: Chat UI
nodes.role: Where users ask and read answers, with the sources linked under each reply.

nodes.label: Feedback signal
nodes.role: Thumbs up/down and wrong-answer flags, collected per reply.

caption: Two sources pass a manual curation gate before indexing. Questions hit the retriever, the LLM answers from retrieved context with citations, and user feedback flows back into the eval set. Dashed = planned: the eval gate does not block deploys yet.
